description: Letter of Benayahu b. Musa, an Alexandrian from Maghribi origin, to Nahray b. Nissim in Fustat. The letter contains severe complaints on the local Alexandrian Jews treatment of foreigners, especially from the Maghrib. The letter sheds light on a big communal dispute which reached the Muslim authorities and almost caused the writer to convert to Islam. The letter mentions in passing Avraham al-Deri as being in Alexandria and, apparently, as being on friendly terms with the writer. Benayahu also conveys his preoccupation for the addressee, who is recovering from an illness (r3–6). (Information from Frenkel)
description_he: מכתב ביהודית-ערבית מאלכסנדריה. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
